commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - The next action is bundle MCP owner review of the SDK interop exception, private cache seam, and overlap with the related quarantine draft.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes untrusted MCP schema parsing but keeps single-response validation, SDK metadata caching, and explicit non-object filtering. (sha=4bf010fa8459a1be19c14e736f70feefee74eac6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes bundled MCP tools/list cataloging to accept missing root `inputSchema.type`, backfill object type, skip explicit non-object roots, preserve SDK tool metadata caching, and add focused runtime tests.

PR surface: Source +102, Tests +406. Total +508 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and `v2026.6.8` still call SDK-strict `client.listTools`, and the pinned SDK rejects missing root `inputSchema.type`; the PR body also supplies live out-of-process stdio MCP before/after output.
commands.review_followup: - The next action is bundle MCP owner review of the SDK interop exception, private cache seam, and overlap with the related quarantine draft.

target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- `listAllTools` validates each `tools/list` page **once** with `RelaxedListToolsResultSchema` and catalogs **that same validated payload** — there is no strict-then-relist flow, so a stateful or hostile server cannot pass validation with one response and have a different response consumed (no validation-to-use gap).
- `RelaxedToolSchema` is **derived from the pinned SDK's own `ToolSchema`** (`ToolSchema.extend` / `ToolSchema.shape.inputSchema.extend`) and differs from it at exactly one point: the root `inputSchema.type` is an optional string instead of `z.literal("object")`. Every other field — `outputSchema`, task `execution` metadata, `annotations`, pagination — keeps the exact pinned SDK contract, so malformed metadata still rejects the page.
- After every page parse, the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` is invoked with the validated tools — the same side effect `client.listTools()` runs — so `callTool()` enforces output-schema validation and task-required guards identically to the SDK flow. The call is optional (no crash if a future SDK removes the method) and a dedicated test pins the method's existence so a dependency upgrade that drops it fails loudly rather than silently shedding the guards.
- `normalizeInputSchema()` backfills `type: "object"` only when the field is missing; `isValidRootInputSchema()` + the catalog gate skip tools with an explicit non-object root type (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) with a `logWarn`. Server `toolCount` reports accepted tools only.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type` and crashes the whole catalog.

## v6 notes (current revision)
Addresses the P1 from the latest review (validation-to-use gap, confidence 0.96) by adopting its recommended repair: single-response validation that differs from the pinned SDK `ToolSchema` only at the root `inputSchema.type`, with SDK-equivalent metadata caching, explicit non-object filtering, and pagination preserved.
- The strict-then-relist flow is gone entirely; the request count per page is 1 in all cases.
- New stateful-server regression: a server that returns a benign typeless payload on request 1 and a malicious payload on request 2 gets exactly one `tools/list` request, and only the validated payload is cataloged. **Mutation-checked**: against the previous strict-then-relist implementation this test fails with the catalog containing the drifted (never-strictly-validated) tool — reproducing the reported attack — and the server log showing two list requests.
- New SDK-seam pin test: `Client.prototype.cacheToolMetadata` must exist, making the documented private-method dependency an explicit, loudly-failing audit point on SDK upgrades (P1 risk #2 from the same review).
- Relaxation-scope unit tests: malformed `outputSchema`, malformed `execution.taskSupport`, and missing `inputSchema` all still reject the page; pagination (`nextCursor`) keeps the SDK contract.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type` by validating each `tools/list` response once against a schema derived from the pinned SDK contract (relaxed only at the root `inputSchema.type`), backfills `type: "object"`, skips explicit non-object roots, keeps servers with unrelated strict failures rejected, never issues a second list request, and preserves the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects for every page.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from the rebased PR head, Node 25.2.1, pnpm 11.2.2, driving the real bundle MCP runtime (`getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime` → `getCatalog()` → `callTool()`) against four real out-of-process stdio MCP servers, one of them stateful (different payload per request).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on both files, plus a live four-server e2e script via `pnpm exec tsx`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  42 passed (42)
pnpm tsgo:core / tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint / oxfmt --check: clean

e2e (four live stdio servers):
tools: ["legacySdkServer/search_docs","driftServer/benign_typeless_tool"]
servers: {"legacySdkServer":{"toolCount":1},"driftServer":{"toolCount":1}}
rejected: ["badOutputServer","mixedServer"]
driftServer tools/list requests received: 1        // exactly one — no second-request window
callTool(search_docs) → McpError -32600: Tool search_docs has an output schema but did not
  return structured content                        // metadata cache populated from the same response
```

Observed result after fix: A `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` catalogs cleanly with `type: "object"` backfilled; an explicit `type: "array"` sibling is skipped with a logWarn; servers with malformed `outputSchema` (alone or mixed with a repairable tool) stay rejected; a stateful server attempting response drift receives exactly one list request and only its validated payload is cataloged; `callTool()` enforces `outputSchema` for typeless tools exactly as the SDK strict flow would.

What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
